title: 10 minutes http client timeout is too low when image generation is done on CPU
summary:
### What is the issue?

[This line](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/x/imagegen/server.go#L58) sets the 10 minute timeout regardless of the hardware.

This is maybe fine on a GPU, but is certainly too low for a CPU.
The timeout should be at least an hour or more when CPU is used.

### Rele

title: Fix File Editing Tool Reliability - replace_in_file, write_to_file, and Diff Failures
summary:
## Problem

Cline's file editing tools (replace_in_file and write_to_file) suffer from widespread reliability issues that significantly impact user productivity and increase API costs. These failures affect users across all models (Claude 3.7/4, Gemini, GPT, local models) and cause frustrating infin

title: Allow importing multi-file GGUF models
summary:
### What is the issue?

Currently Ollama can [import GGUF files](https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/import.md). However, larger models are sometimes split into separate files. Ollama should support loading multiple GGUF files similar to loading safetensor files.

title: [PR] feat(skills): add cron-model-fallback skill
summary:
## Summary

Adds a **cron model fallback chain** skill that tries models in priority order until one succeeds. Solves the problem of silent cron job failures when a model provider is unavailable.

## Problem

OpenClaw cron jobs accept a single `model` string. If that model is unreachable, the job fa

title: [PR] Fix production embedder: configurable provider for Fly.io deployment
summary:
## Summary
- Add `EMBEDDER_PROVIDER` env var to select between `ollama` (local dev, default) and `google` (production)
- When `google`, uses `gemini-embedding-001` via the existing `LLM_API_KEY` — no new API key needed
- Add `google-generativeai` to `requirements.txt` for the embedder package

title: [PR] jetbrains.jdk: 21.0.9-1163.86 -> 25.0.2-b329.72
summary:
Updates the Jetbrains Runtime from Java 21 to  Java 25. The Jetbrains IDEs packages default to JBR as the JDK, updating the Jetbrains JDK to Java 25 allows the IDEs to have access to Java 25 by default. JBR is also often used for developing with DCEVM.
